title: Empty State
description: Empty State communicates that there is currently no content to show and helps the user understand what to do next through concise copy, optional icons, actions, and custom content.
intro: Use `ui-empty-state` for empty lists, first-run experiences, no-results states, and permission-related blanks. Focus on clear explanation and the next sensible step instead of decorative filler.

featureSections.title: Basic empty messaging
featureSections.description: The default pattern is short, calm, and direct. Most empty states do not need more than a clear title and one sentence of context.

featureSections.title: Icons and sizes
featureSections.description: Icons add context and sizes help the component fit compact panels or larger first-run surfaces. Use them to support the message, not to overpower it.

featureSections.title: Primary and secondary actions
featureSections.description: Actions are useful when the next step is obvious, such as inviting users, importing data, or creating the first record.

featureSections.title: Custom projected content
featureSections.description: Projected content helps when the empty state needs richer explanation, setup guidance, or a slightly more tailored layout.

featureSections.title: Common empty-state scenarios
featureSections.description: Different causes of emptiness need different copy. A no-results state should not read like a first-run state or a permissions block.

featureSections.title: Embedded list or panel layout
featureSections.description: Empty states often sit inside larger application shells rather than on a blank page. This is the pattern users usually encounter in practice.

accessibility.title: Content clarity
accessibility.body: An empty state is primarily explanatory content. The title and description should clearly communicate why nothing is shown and what the user can do next.

accessibility.title: Actions
accessibility.body:
If actions are present, they should have clear visible labels and remain keyboard reachable like any other buttons.

| Element | Guidance |
| --- | --- |
| Primary action | Use when there is one obvious next step. |
| Secondary action | Use for alternate paths such as import, learn more, or view docs. |
| Custom projected controls | Keep them simple and semantically clear. |

accessibility.title: Decorative icons
accessibility.body: Icons in empty states are supporting visuals. They should reinforce meaning but not be the only way the state is understood.

apiSections.title: EmptyStateComponent - ui-empty-state
apiSections.lead: Standalone component for presenting a no-content state with concise messaging, optional iconography, optional actions, and projected custom content.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Optional icon that adds visual context.
Overall scale of the empty state.

blocks.title: Projected content
rows: Optional projected custom content rendered under the main message.
{ label, variant?, appearance?, icon?, size?, disabled?, action? }
Action contract used by primary and secondary buttons.

paragraphs: Keep empty-state copy specific to the cause of the absence. 'No results', 'Nothing created yet', and 'No permission' are different situations and should not reuse the same wording.
Use projected custom content sparingly. If the state becomes too dense, it usually means the surrounding flow should be redesigned rather than making the empty state more complicated.
blocks.title: Notes
